title: Upgrade live game cards with clearer at-bat state, next-three-up, and baseball-first visual hierarchy
status: QUEUED — do not start until DASHBOARD_TRUTH_REVERIFY_001 result is reviewed and closed
sequencing_note: DASHBOARD_TRUTH_REVERIFY_001 is currently running against dashboard.html and dashboard_server.py. This task touches the same files. Do not pick this up until the reverify is approved — we must confirm the current truth baseline before layering new UI on top of it.
objective: Improve the live game cards so an operator can instantly understand what is happening in the game: score, inning, outs, count, runners, current batter/pitcher, and next three batters due up. Prefer a dashboard-only UI polish. Only add a minimal dashboard payload/server change if the next-three-up data is not already exposed to the client.
type: ui-first polish with minimal data-enrichment fallback
context: The operator wants the live game cards to feel more like a live baseball game tracker and less like a plain status block.
Requested presentation targets: next three up to bat, current hitter, balls, strikes, outs, clearer visual game-state, color-coded count/status, and an overall prettier live card.
Keep repo inspection narrow. Do not do a broad repo sweep.

The board already has dashboard-related verification/fix work in the queue, including DASHBOARD_TRUTH_REVERIFY_001 and DASHBOARD_ESPN_RELIABILITY_001, so this task must stay tightly scoped to live-card presentation and only the smallest required data exposure.
Current runtime notes indicate the stack needed restart to pick up prior risk-pack changes, so do not mix this task with risk or process-management work.
manager_intent: Assume dashboard.html is the primary file.
Touch dashboard_server.py only if the worker proves the client does not already receive the needed live-game fields.
Do not widen into bot logic, model logic, risk logic, or process-management work.
allowed_files: C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\dashboard_server.py — only if strictly required to expose next_three_up or other already-known live-game fields to the client
One tiny helper/style block directly used by dashboard.html if such a file already exists and is directly imported by dashboard.html
do_not_touch: unrelated dashboard sections outside the live game cards unless required for card rendering consistency
required_read_scope: dashboard_server.py only if needed to confirm available fields
One live payload sample or current rendered live-card function path if needed to confirm available fields
design_targets: Current at-bat section with batter, pitcher, inning half, outs, and count
Color-coded count display for balls, strikes, and outs
Clear base-runner diamond or equivalent occupied-base indicator
Next Up section showing the next 3 batters due
Bigger score / inning hierarchy so game state is readable at a glance
Preserve existing trading/position context, but visually secondary to live baseball state
Use compact, readable chips/badges/cards instead of dense plain text
fallback_rule: If next-three-up is already present in the live payload, implement UI only.
If next-three-up is not present but the source data is already available server-side, add the smallest server exposure needed.

If the worker cannot confirm that next-three-up is already available anywhere in the current dashboard path, stop and report that a separate payload-enrichment task is required instead of inventing a large backend change.
deliverables: Before/after description of the live card layout
List of which live-game fields were already available vs newly exposed
If next-three-up was unavailable, explicit note describing the smallest follow-on task needed
acceptance_criteria: Live game card clearly shows score, inning/half, outs, and ball-strike count
Next three up are displayed if already available or minimally exposable
Runner/base state is easier to read than before
UI is meaningfully prettier and more readable without breaking existing card content
No unrelated repo files are touched
No bot logic, risk logic, or process-management behavior is changed
verification: Open live dashboard card and confirm count, outs, and inning are readable at a glance
Confirm current batter/pitcher display renders without breaking existing live card
Confirm next-three-up appears correctly when data exists
Confirm existing score/game state still updates
Confirm position/trade context still appears and is not removed
Confirm no unrelated components visibly regress
rollback: Revert only the touched dashboard UI/payload changes. No schema or runtime rollback should be necessary if scope is respected.
worker_notes: Do not redesign the whole dashboard.
If you need data-contract confirmation, inspect only the render path and the exact server endpoint feeding the live game cards.
If you discover this is really two tasks (UI polish + payload enrichment), finish only the clearly scoped portion and report the second task cleanly.
